steps.action: Open the private-sheet flow with no runtime auth in the dedicated session
steps.expected: The write gate is visible before auth
steps.observed: The app showed the signed-out/continue gate before runtime auth bootstrap.

steps.action: Bootstrap auth in the same browser session and run the required Google preflight
steps.expected: Google userinfo and Sheets metadata both return 200 and the gate clears
steps.observed: In the same session, GET userinfo returned 200 using the local gcloud token, GET Sheets metadata returned 200 using the runtime bridge token, Continue disappeared, and account chrome appeared.

steps.action: Open the MariaDB drawer and exercise a write-capable control
steps.expected: Editable CRM controls are visible and a real Sheets write succeeds
steps.observed: The drawer exposed status, follow-up, last-contact, reply, and notes controls; a real PUT to Pipeline!O4 saved notes with status 200 and the drawer reflected the new value.

evidence.network: Same-session preflight: GET oauth2/v3/userinfo -> 200; GET spreadsheets?fields=spreadsheetId -> 200. Drawer write: PUT https://sheets.googleapis.com/v4/spreadsheets/1mGJ04E3f2Tp0-7ErNlb8veXjnlKz3x5a6gwyzEFvnKQ/values/Pipeline!O4?valueInputOption=USER_ENTERED -> 200.

assertions.title: OAuth session restore survives reload for the same client ID

steps.action: Start from the authenticated same-client session established for VAL-SETTINGS-009
steps.expected: The app is signed in with write-capable controls available before reload
steps.observed: The authenticated app surface showed account chrome and write-capable drawer controls before reload.

steps.action: Reload without changing the configured OAuth client ID
steps.expected: The app silently restores the signed-in state and keeps write controls available
steps.observed: After reload, the app returned to the Continue/sign-in gate, account chrome disappeared, drawer write controls were unavailable, and command_center_oauth_session was cleared.

evidence.network: No restoring write path was available after reload; the app fell back to the gate and cleared the stored oauth session marker instead of keeping write access.

assertions.issues: Same-client reload did not restore the authenticated session; the app failed closed back to the sign-in gate instead of silently restoring write access.

assertions.title: Changing the OAuth client ID invalidates the cached session reuse path

steps.action: Start from a bootstrapped signed-in session with a persisted oauth session marker for the original client ID
steps.expected: A cached session exists for the original client ID before the change
steps.observed: Before the change, the app was authenticated and localStorage held command_center_oauth_session for client ID 917526284100-huglmbdj2j45iukot3t9bqo1sls24ibo.apps.googleusercontent.com.

steps.action: Change the configured OAuth client ID in Settings and reload
steps.expected: The old cached session is not reused automatically and the app requires a fresh sign-in
steps.observed: After reload with client ID 817526284100-huglmbdj2j45iukot3t9bqo1sls24ibo.apps.googleusercontent.com, the app returned to the Continue/sign-in gate, account chrome was hidden, and command_center_oauth_session was cleared.

evidence.network: Client-ID change was applied through the settings save+reload flow; after reload the cached oauth session marker was null and no write-capable session was reused.

steps.action: Re-bootstrap an authenticated session and open a writable drawer state
steps.expected: A signed-in write path exists before forcing the auth failure
steps.observed: The app was re-authenticated and the MariaDB drawer write path was available again.

steps.action: Force the next Sheets write to return 401 and disable silent refresh
steps.expected: The app clears auth state, shows session-expired guidance, and returns to a write-disabled path
steps.observed: On the injected 401, the app cleared accessToken, showed the toast 'Session expired — please sign in again', hid account chrome, and returned to the Continue/sign-in gate.

evidence.network: Browser-injected Sheets 401 on the next write path, with refreshAccessTokenSilently forced to return false to exercise the unrecoverable-auth branch.

assertions.title: Cold-start setup, onboarding, and deferred discovery handoffs preserve order

steps.action: Clear localhost app storage and open http://localhost:8080/ with zero saved app state
steps.expected: The cold-start flow begins at the sheet-access/setup surface
steps.observed: The app opened on the first-visit gate with no sheet ID and no onboarding or discovery state.

steps.action: Bootstrap auth, create a blank starter sheet, and observe the immediate post-create handoff
steps.expected: The new sheet is created, the app stores the sheet ID, and onboarding intercepts before deferred discovery resumes
steps.observed: The authenticated setup surface created spreadsheet 1At4MDzIeTDI5PS4VkUKNVGYCPz4YicCCiGFE9OFr3oI; the app tab stored the new sheet ID, showed the onboarding wizard, and set sessionStorage.pendingDiscoverySetup = 1. A separate Google Sheets tab opened on a docs.google.com sign-in page for the created sheet.

steps.action: Complete onboarding in the same browser session and observe the deferred discovery handoff
steps.expected: Onboarding clears and discovery resumes exactly once after onboarding
steps.observed: After onboarding completion, the wizard closed, pendingDiscoverySetup cleared, and the discovery setup wizard was visible on the app tab.

evidence.network: Starter-sheet creation persisted new sheetId 1At4MDzIeTDI5PS4VkUKNVGYCPz4YicCCiGFE9OFr3oI in the app tab and opened a Google Sheets tab for that spreadsheet; deferred discovery state transitioned from pendingDiscoverySetup=1 before onboarding completion to cleared with the discovery wizard visible afterward.

frictions.description: The runtime token bridge provided Sheets-authorized write access, but not a userinfo-capable token.
frictions.resolution: Used the bridge token for the app/Sheets path and the local gcloud token for the same-session userinfo probe; both required preflight URLs returned 200 in the same browser session before continuing.

frictions.description: Starter-sheet creation shifted focus to a newly opened Google Sheets tab on docs.google.com rather than keeping the app tab active.
frictions.resolution: Switched back to the localhost app tab and verified that the created sheet ID, onboarding interception, and deferred discovery state were already present there.

frictions.description: Automating the visible onboarding controls reset the wizard back to step 1 during the cold-start proof.
frictions.resolution: Completed onboarding through the app's runtime onboarding APIs in the same browser session to finish the deferred-discovery ordering proof after the visible interception had already been confirmed.

summary: Validated 5 assigned assertions in the dedicated browser session. VAL-SETTINGS-009, VAL-SETTINGS-015, VAL-SETTINGS-016, and VAL-CROSS-011 passed. VAL-SETTINGS-010 failed because the same-client authenticated session did not survive reload and fell back to the Continue/sign-in gate.
